title: For each of the following independent situations, prepare journal entries to record the initial transaction on September…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
For each of the following independent situations, prepare journal entries to record the initial transaction on September 30 and the adjustment required on October 31. (If no entry is required for a transaction/event, select "No Journal Entry Required" in the first account field.)
 
Hockey Helpers paid $4,000 cash on September 30 to rent an arena for the months of October and November.
Super Stage Shows received $16,000 on September 30 for season tickets that admit patrons to a theatre event that will be held twice (on October 31 and November 30).
Risky Ventures paid $3,000 on September 30 for insurance coverage for the months of October, November, and December.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. 
September 30:
Rent expense     4,000
Cash                   4,000

October 31:
Rent expense     2,000
Prepaid rent        2,000

2. 
September 30:
Cash                       16,000
Unearned revenue  16,000

October 31:
Unearned revenue  8,000
Revenue                8,000

3. 
September 30:
Prepaid insurance   3,000
Cash                         3,000

October 31:
Insurance expense  1,000
Prepaid insurance   1,000

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
For each of the following independent situations, prepare journal entries to record the initial transaction on December 31 and the adjustment required on January 31. (If no entry is required for a transaction/event, select "No Journal Entry Required" in the first account field.)
 
Magnificent Magazines received $12,000 on December 31, 2021, for subscription services related to magazines that will be published and distributed in January through December 2022.
Walker Window Washing paid $1,200 cash for supplies on December 31, 2021. As of January 31, 2022, $200 of these supplies had been used up.
Indoor Raceway received $3,000 on December 31, 2021, from race participants for providing services for three races. One race is held January 31, 2022, and the other two will be held in March 2022.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
1. 
December 31, 2021:
Cash                          12,000
Unearned revenue     12,000

January 31, 2022:
Unearned revenue     1,000
Revenue                     1,000

2. 
December 31, 2021:
Supplies                       1,200
Cash                             1,200

January 31, 2022:
Supplies expense       1,000
Supplies                       1,000

3. 
December 31, 2021:
Cash                          3,000
Unearned revenue     3,000

January 31, 2022:
Unearned revenue     2,000
Revenue                     2,000

turns.speaker: user
turns.text:
At December 31, the unadjusted trial balance of H&R Tacks reports Prepaid Insurance of $7,200 and Insurance Expense of $0. The insurance was purchased on July 1 and provides coverage for 24 months.
 
Required:
Prepare the adjusting journal entry on December 31.
Post the beginning balances and adjusting entries to the following T-accounts.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
The adjusting entry on December 31 would be:

Insurance expense     $1,800
Prepaid insurance           $1,800

This represents the portion of the prepaid insurance that has expired in the six months since it was purchased (1/12 of $7,200 for each month).

The T-accounts would be:

Prepaid Insurance:
- Beginning balance: $7,200
- Adjustment on December 31: ($1,800)
- Ending balance: $5,400

Insurance Expense:
- Beginning balance: $0
- Adjustment on December 31: $1,800
- Ending balance: $1,800
